message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
In the project root, can you create a README.md file with project overview and setup and run instructions

input.prompt:
Explore the claims-processing-system project at /Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system. I need to understand:

1. The full directory structure (list all top-level files and folders)
2. Contents of: CLAUDE.md, docs/domain-concepts.md, docs/acceptance-criteria.md, pyproject.toml
3. The app/ directory structure
4. Any existing config files (config/)
5. How the app is run (look for main entry points, run scripts, Makefile, etc.)
6. Any .env.example or similar files showing required environment variables
7. The tests/ directory structure

Please read all relevant files and report back the complete contents so I can write a comprehensive README.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Project Exploration Complete

I've thoroughly explored the claims-processing-system project. Here's the complete information you requested:

---

## 1. Full Directory Structure

**Top-level files and folders:**
```
claims-processing-system/
├── app/                          # Application code
│   ├── __init__.py              # Flask app factory (create_app)
│   ├── extensions.py            # SQLAlchemy + Migrate initialization
│   ├── errors.py                # Custom error classes & handlers
│   ├── models.py                # All 12 ORM models in one file
│   ├── routes/                  # API blueprints
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── pages.py            # Frontend pages (/, /claim, /admin)
│   │   ├── members.py          # /api/members endpoints
│   │   ├── providers.py        # /api/providers endpoints
│   │   ├── plans.py            # /api/plans endpoints
│   │   ├── policies.py         # /api/policies endpoints
│   │   └── claims.py           # /api/claims endpoints (includes disputes)
│   ├── services/                # Business logic
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── claim_service.py    # Claim submission & retrieval
│   │   ├── adjudication_engine.py # Core adjudication logic
│   │   └── dispute_service.py  # Dispute & re-adjudication
│   └── static/                  # Frontend assets
│       ├── index.html          # Dashboard (member lookup, claims list)
│       ├── claim.html          # Claim detail page
│       ├── admin.html          # Admin panel (plans, policies, disputes)
│       ├── css/
│       │   └── styles.css      # 316 lines
│       └── js/
│           ├── api.js          # Fetch wrapper (43 lines)
│           ├── dashboard.js    # Dashboard logic (224 lines)
│           ├── claim-detail.js # Claim detail logic (297 lines)
│           └── admin.js        # Admin panel logic (318 lines)
├── tests/
│   ├── conftest.py            # pytest fixtures (app, db, client, seed data)
│   ├── unit/                   # Unit tests
│   │   ├── test_adjudication_engine.py
│   │   ├── test_claim_service.py
│   │   ├── test_dispute_service.py
│   │   └── test_state_machine.py
│   └── integration/            # Integration tests
│       ├── test_members_api.py
│       ├── test_providers_api.py
│       ├── test_plans_api.py
│       ├── test_policies_api.py
│       ├── test_claims_api.py
│       └── test_disputes_api.py
├── config/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── settings.py            # Config and TestingConfig classes
├── migrations/                # Flask-Migrate (Alembic) managed
│   ├── alembic.ini
│   ├── env.py
│   ├── script.py.mako
│   └── versions/              # Migration files
├── instance/                  # Flask instance folder (ignored)
├── .claude/                   # Claude Code metadata
├── .venv/                     # Python virtual environment
├── .git/                      # Git repository
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Project guidelines & standards
├── pyproject.toml            # Python project configuration
├── uv.lock                   # Dependency lock file
└── .gitignore               # Git ignore patterns
```

---

## 2. CLAUDE.md (Project Guidelines)

**Purpose:** Project overview and code standards

**Stack:**
- Language: Python 3.13
- Backend API: Flask
- Database: SQLite
- ORM: Flask-SQLAlchemy
- Migrations: Flask-Migrate (Alembic)
- Frontend: Vanilla JavaScript
- Tooling: Astral ecosystem (uv, ruff)

**Code Standards:**
- Type hints on all function signatures
- One module = one responsibility
- No premature abstractions
- No hardcoded secrets or magic numbers
- Google-style docstrings on all public functions
- Use `logging`, never `print()`
- No bare `except:` — always catch specific exceptions

**Testing Requirements:**
- Test-first (write tests before code)
- Run tests before presenting work
- Run `ruff format`, `ruff check`, and `mypy` before finishing
- Check if existing tests need updating when making changes

**Git:**
- Conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:)
- Suggest commit messages after changes

**Documentation:**
- Document scope and assumptions in `docs/decisions.md`

---

## 3. docs/domain-concepts.md (Domain Model)

**Key Entities:**
- **Member**: Individual insured person (one active policy, one insurance)
- **Provider**: Healthcare professional/facility (identified by NPI)
- **Plan**: Template defining deductible, CPT coverage rules, and percentages
- **Policy**: Member's instance of a plan with start/end dates
- **Claim**: Request for reimbursement with 1+ line items
- **LineItem**: Single billable service (diagnosis code [ICD-10], procedure code [CPT], billed amount)
- **Adjudication Result**: Outcome of evaluating a line item (covered?, deductible, plan pays, member owes, explanation)
- **Accumulator**: Running total of deductible met per member per policy period

**Coverage Rules:**
- Defined at CPT code level with coverage percentage
- Deductible applied first, then coinsurance

**Adjudication Math (per line item):**
```
remaining_deductible = deductible - deductible_met_so_far
applied_to_deductible = min(billed_amount, remaining_deductible)
amount_after_deductible = billed_amount - applied_to_deductible
plan_pays = amount_after_deductible * coverage_percentage
member_owes = applied_to_deductible + (amount_after_deductible * (1 - coverage_percentage))
```

**Claim Lifecycle:**
```
submitted → under_review → approved / denied / partially_approved → paid
```

**Disputes:**
- Member may dispute `denied` or `partially_approved` claim once
- Returns claim to `under_review` for re-adjudication
- Cannot be disputed more than once

---

## 4. docs/acceptance-criteria.md (Requirements)

**Functional Requirements:**
1. **Claim Submission**: Accept claims with 1+ line items, validate inputs, return claim ID with `submitted` status
2. **Coverage Adjudication**: Evaluate line items independently, apply deductible + coinsurance, return explanations
3. **Claim Lifecycle**: State machine with transitions logged and timestamped
4. **Decision Transparency**: Human-readable explanations for every adjudication
5. **Disputes**: One-time dispute mechanism with re-adjudication capability
6. **Policy & Coverage Rules**: Define covered services, percentages, deductible, policy period
7. **API Correctness**: HTTP status codes (200, 201, 400, 404, 422, 500), JSON responses
8. **Data Integrity**: 2 decimal places for monetary amounts, single-user/low-concurrency (SQLite limitation)
9. **Observability**: INFO-level state transition logs, ERROR logs with context
10. **Non-Functional**: 2-second response time target, comprehensive test coverage

---

## 5. pyproject.toml (Project Configuration)

```toml
[project]
name = "claims-processing-system"
version = "0.1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.13"
dependencies = [
    "flask>=3.1.0",
    "flask-sqlalchemy>=3.1.1",
    "flask-migrate>=4.0.7",
]

[tool.uv]
dev-dependencies = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-flask>=1.3.0",
    "mypy>=1.13.0",
    "ruff>=0.8.0",
    "types-flask-migrate>=4.1.0.20250809",
]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py313"
line-length = 100

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "UP"]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.13"
strict = true
plugins = ["sqlalchemy.ext.mypy.plugin"]
```

---

## 6. app/ Directory Structure

**Core Files:**

- **`__init__.py`**: Contains `create_app()` factory function
  - Initializes Flask app with Decimal JSON provider (for monetary values)
  - Registers database, migrations, routes, and error handlers
  - Takes optional `config_object` parameter (defaults to `Config`)

- **`extensions.py`**: Exports `db` (SQLAlchemy) and `migrate` (Flask-Migrate)
  - Defines `Base` declarative base for all models

- **`models.py`** (~460 lines):
  - 6 Enums: `ProviderType`, `PolicyStatus`, `ClaimStatus`, `ReviewType`, `LineItemStatus`, `DisputeStatus`
  - 12 Models: `Member`, `Provider`, `Plan`, `CoverageRule`, `Policy`, `Accumulator`, `Claim`, `ClaimStatusHistory`, `LineItem`, `AdjudicationResult`, `Dispute`, `Payment`

- **`errors.py`**: Custom exception classes & error handlers
  - `ClaimsError` (base), `BadRequestError` (400), `NotFoundError` (404), `ValidationError` (422), `ConflictError` (409), `ForbiddenError` (403)
  - `register_error_handlers()` function for JSON error responses

**API Routes (Blueprints):**

- **`routes/__init__.py`**: Registers all blueprints
- **`routes/pages.py`**: Frontend pages (/, /claim, /admin)
- **`routes/members.py`**: CRUD endpoints for members
- **`routes/providers.py`**: CRUD endpoints for providers
- **`routes/plans.py`**: CRUD endpoints for plans + coverage rules
- **`routes/policies.py`**: CRUD endpoints for policies
- **`routes/claims.py`**: Submit claims, retrieve claims, adjudication results

**Services (Business Logic):**

- **`services/claim_service.py`**: Validate & persist claims, trigger adjudication
- **`services/adjudication_engine.py`**: Core adjudication algorithm (deductible + coinsurance)
- **`services/dispute_service.py`**: Dispute submission & re-adjudication

---

## 7. config/ Directory

**`config/settings.py`:**
```python
class Config:
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI: str = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///claims.db")
    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS: bool = False

class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING: bool = True
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI: str = "sqlite:///:memory:"
```

**Environment Variables:**
- `DATABASE_URL`: SQLite database path (defaults to `sqlite:///claims.db`)

**No `.env.example` file exists** — only `DATABASE_URL` is needed (has a sensible default).

---

## 8. How to Run the App

**Entry Point:**
- Flask app factory: `app.create_app()` in `app/__init__.py`
- No explicit `wsgi.py` or `app.py` file in root
- Use Flask CLI: `flask --app app:create_app run`

**Database Setup:**
1. Virtual environment: `.venv/` (Python 3.13)
2. Install dependencies: `uv sync`
3. Initialize DB: `flask --app app:create_app db upgrade`
4. Run server: `flask --app app:create_app run`

**Frontend:**
- Static files served from `app/static/`
- Routes available:
  - `/` — Member dashboard
  - `/claim` — Claim detail page
  - `/admin` — Admin panel
  - `/api/*` — API endpoints

---

## 9. tests/ Directory Structure

**Unit Tests:**
- `test_adjudication_engine.py` — Adjudication math, deductible tracking
- `test_claim_service.py` — Claim validation and submission
- `test_dispute_service.py` — Dispute and re-adjudication guards
- `test_state_machine.py` — Claim state transitions

**Integration Tests:**
- `test_members_api.py` — Member CRUD endpoints
- `test_providers_api.py` — Provider CRUD endpoints
- `test_plans_api.py` — Plan + CoverageRule endpoints
- `test_policies_api.py` — Policy CRUD endpoints
- `test_claims_api.py` — Claim submission, retrieval, adjudication
- `test_disputes_api.py` — Dispute submission, re-adjudication

**Test Setup:**
- `conftest.py` — Fixtures for Flask app, database, client, and seed data
- Database: In-memory SQLite for tests (`sqlite:///:memory:`)
- Run: `pytest` (configured in `pyproject.toml`)

---

## 10. Additional Documentation

**`docs/decisions.md`:**
Documents design choices including:
- Plan as template (multiple members per plan)
- Single deductible per plan, tracked cumulatively
- Line-item-level adjudication
- Revision-based history (append-only adjudication results)
- State machine with timestamped transitions
- One-time dispute mechanism
- NPI as provider identifier (US-specific)
- Explicit out-of-scope items (family plans, COB, prior auth, OOP max, etc.)

**`docs/implementation-plan.md`:**
Detailed phased implementation guide covering scaffolding, models, routes, services, tests, and frontend.

---

## Summary for README

The project is a **Flask-based insurance claims processing system** with:
- **Backend**: Flask API with SQLAlchemy ORM and SQLite database
- **Frontend**: Vanilla JavaScript dashboard for members + admin panel
- **Core Logic**: Line-item adjudication with deductible tracking and dispute resolution
- **Testing**: Comprehensive unit and integration tests with pytest
- **Standards**: Type hints, docstrings, linting (ruff), type checking (mypy)
- **State Management**: Claim lifecycle state machine with audit trail
- **Extensibility**: Modular services, clear separation of concerns

All relevant absolute file paths have been identified for your README development.

- `conftest.py` — Fixtures for Flask app, database, client, and seed data
- Database: In-memory SQLite for tests (`sqlite:///:memory:`)
- Run: `pytest` (configured in `pyproject.toml`)

---

## 10. Additional Documentation

**`docs/decisions.md`:**
Documents design choices including:
- Plan as template (multiple members per plan)
- Single deductible per plan, tracked cumulatively
- Line-item-level adjudication
- Revision-based history (append-only adjudication results)
- State machine with timestamped transitions
- One-time dispute mechanism
- NPI as provider identifier (US-specific)
- Explicit out-of-scope items (family plans, COB, prior auth, OOP max, etc.)

**`docs/implementation-plan.md`:**
Detailed phased implementation guide covering scaffolding, models, routes, services, tests, and frontend.

---

## Summary for README

The project is a **Flask-based insurance claims processing system** with:
- **Backend**: Flask API with SQLAlchemy ORM and SQLite database
- **Frontend**: Vanilla JavaScript dashboard for members + admin panel
- **Core Logic**: Line-item adjudication with deductible tracking and dispute resolution
- **Testing**: Comprehensive unit and integration tests with pytest
- **Standards**: Type hints, docstrings, linting (ruff), type checking (mypy)
- **State Management**: Claim lifecycle state machine with audit trail
- **Extensibility**: Modular services, clear separation of concerns

All relevant absolute file paths have been identified for your README development.
Now I have everything I need. Let me write the plan.

# Plan: Create README.md for Claims Processing System

## Context
The project has no README.md. The user wants a project overview plus setup/run instructions so that anyone cloning the repo can understand what the system does and get it running quickly.

## File to Create
- `/Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system/README.md`

## README Structure

### 1. Title & one-liner description
### 2. Overview
- What the system does (insurance claims processing: submit, adjudicate, track lifecycle, dispute)
- Key entities: Member, Provider, Plan, Policy, Claim, LineItem

### 3. Features
- Claim submission with multi-line-item support
- Deductible + coinsurance adjudication engine
- Claim lifecycle state machine (submitted → under_review → approved/denied/partially_approved → paid)
- One-time dispute mechanism with re-adjudication
- Web UI: member dashboard, claim detail, admin panel
- REST API

### 4. Tech Stack table (from CLAUDE.md)

### 5. Project Structure (abbreviated directory tree)

### 6. Prerequisites
- Python 3.13+
- uv (Astral)

### 7. Setup & Run Instructions
```bash
# Clone & enter project
cd claims-processing-system

# Install dependencies (creates .venv automatically)
uv sync

# Apply database migrations
uv run flask --app app:create_app db upgrade

# Start the development server
uv run flask --app app:create_app run
```
- App at http://127.0.0.1:5000
- Pages: `/` (dashboard), `/claim` (claim detail), `/admin` (admin panel)
- API: `/api/*`

### 8. Environment Variables
| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| DATABASE_URL | sqlite:///claims.db | SQLite database path |

### 9. Running Tests
```bash
uv run pytest                   # all tests
uv run pytest tests/unit/       # unit only
uv run pytest tests/integration # integration only
```

### 10. Code Quality
```bash
uv run ruff format .    # format
uv run ruff check .     # lint
uv run mypy app/        # type check
```

### 11. Documentation links (brief)
- docs/domain-concepts.md — domain model & adjudication math
- docs/acceptance-criteria.md — functional requirements
- docs/decisions.md — design decisions & scope

## Verification
- Read the generated README.md to confirm accuracy
- No code changes; no tests to run

input.content:
# Claims Processing System

An insurance claims processing system that handles claim submission, coverage adjudication, lifecycle tracking, and dispute resolution.

## Overview

Members submit healthcare claims for reimbursement. The system evaluates each claim against the member's active policy, applies deductible and coinsurance rules per line item, and tracks the claim through its full lifecycle.

**Key entities:**

- **Member** — the insured person
- **Provider** — healthcare professional or facility (identified by NPI)
- **Plan** — template defining coverage rules, deductible, and coinsurance percentages
- **Policy** — a member's active instance of a plan with a policy period
- **Claim** — a reimbursement request containing one or more line items
- **LineItem** — a single billed service (CPT procedure code, ICD-10 diagnosis code, billed amount)

## Features

- Claim submission with one or more line items
- Line-item adjudication: deductible tracking + coinsurance calculation
- Claim lifecycle state machine: `submitted` → `under_review` → `approved` / `denied` / `partially_approved` → `paid`
- One-time dispute mechanism with re-adjudication
- Human-readable explanation for every adjudication decision
- Web UI: member dashboard, claim detail view, admin panel
- REST API (`/api/*`)

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Python 3.13 |
| Backend API | Flask |
| Database | SQLite |
| ORM | Flask-SQLAlchemy |
| Migrations | Flask-Migrate (Alembic) |
| Frontend | Vanilla JavaScript |
| Tooling | uv, ruff |

## Project Structure

```
claims-processing-system/
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py          # Flask app factory
│   ├── models.py            # ORM models
│   ├── extensions.py        # SQLAlchemy + Migrate setup
│   ├── errors.py            # Custom errors & handlers
│   ├── routes/              # API blueprints (members, providers, plans, policies, claims)
│   ├── services/            # Business logic (adjudication, claims, disputes)
│   └── static/              # Frontend (HTML, CSS, JS)
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/                # Unit tests for services
│   └── integration/         # API integration tests
├── config/
│   └── settings.py          # Config classes
├── migrations/              # Alembic migration files
└── docs/                    # Domain concepts, acceptance criteria, decisions
```

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.13+
- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) package manager

## Setup & Run

```bash
# 1. Install dependencies (creates .venv automatically)
uv sync

# 2. Apply database migrations
uv run flask --app app:create_app db upgrade

# 3. Start the development server
uv run flask --app app:create_app run
```

The app is available at **http://127.0.0.1:5000**.

| Path | Description |
|---|---|
| `/` | Member dashboard — look up members and view their claims |
| `/claim` | Claim detail — adjudication results and dispute submission |
| `/admin` | Admin panel — manage plans, policies, and disputes |
| `/api/*` | REST API |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DATABASE_URL` | `sqlite:///claims.db` | SQLite database file path |

Create a `.env` file in the project root to override defaults:

```
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///custom.db
```

## Running Tests

```bash
uv run pytest                    # all tests
uv run pytest tests/unit/        # unit tests only
uv run pytest tests/integration/ # integration tests only
```

## Code Quality

```bash
uv run ruff format .   # auto-format
uv run ruff check .    # lint
uv run mypy app/       # type check
```

## Documentation

- [`docs/domain-concepts.md`](docs/domain-concepts.md) — domain model, adjudication math, claim lifecycle
- [`docs/acceptance-criteria.md`](docs/acceptance-criteria.md) — functional requirements and scope
- [`docs/decisions.md`](docs/decisions.md) — design decisions, assumptions, and out-of-scope items
